name: Australia
intro: Australia's project profession is formal, well paid and chronically undersupplied: infrastructure pipelines in every state, banks under permanent regulatory transformation, and a resources sector that runs some of the world's largest capital programs. Australian employers benchmark salaries against certification openly, and PMP plus Scrum credentials both appear directly in seek-listing requirements. Live online batches run year-round with sensible Australia timings — a full Saturday–Sunday weekend cohort plus a midweek Wednesday–Thursday option — so you train during comfortable local daytime hours; the schedule table below lists every upcoming date with its exact Australia start and finish. The package throughout: live weekend instruction, exam application support, full-length mocks, and trainer access until you pass. Group bookings for Australian delivery teams are available with consolidated invoicing across states.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Australia?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Australia?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Australia.
